messages.content:
In the VBA code below the following lines
Case Is >= 4 And Is <= 9: targetSheet = "Week"
    Case Is >= 10 And Is <= 20: targetSheet = "Forthnight"
    Case Is >= 21 And Is <= 59: targetSheet = "Month"
    Case Is >= 60 And Is <= 89: targetSheet = "QtrYear"
    Case Is >= 90 And Is <= 209: targetSheet = "HalfYear"
have synatax errors can you please correct this.


Private Sub CommandButton1_Click()
' Get data from form
Dim desc As String: desc = Me.TaskDescription.Value
Dim TaskType As String: TaskType = Me.TaskType.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
Dim Collaborators As String: Collaborators = Me.Collaborators.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
Dim Resources As String: Resources = Me.Resources.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
Dim priority As String: priority = Me.TaskPriority.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
Dim compDate As Date: compDate = Me.CompletionDate.Value
Dim stages As String: stages = Me.StagesRequired.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing

' Calculate difference in days
Dim diffDays As Long: diffDays = DateDiff("d", Date, compDate)

' Determine target sheet based on difference in days
Dim targetSheet As String
Select Case diffDays
    Case Is <= 3: targetSheet = "Day"
    Case Is >= 4 And Is <= 9: targetSheet = "Week"
    Case Is >= 10 And Is <= 20: targetSheet = "Forthnight"
    Case Is >= 21 And Is <= 59: targetSheet = "Month"
    Case Is >= 60 And Is <= 89: targetSheet = "QtrYear"
    Case Is >= 90 And Is <= 209: targetSheet = "HalfYear"
    Case Else: targetSheet = "Year"
End Select

' Find next available row
Dim lastRow As Long: lastRow = Sheets(targetSheet).Cells.Find("*", SearchOrder = xlByRows, SearchDirection = xlPrevious).Row + 1

' Write data to sheet
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 1).Value = desc
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 2).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(TaskType, 1).Value
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 3).Value = Me.TaskDescription
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 4).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(Collaborators, 1).Value
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 5).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(Resources, 1).Value
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 6).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(priority, 1).Value
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 7).Value = compDate
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 8).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(stages, 1).Value

' Display confirmation message
MsgBox "Your new task has been recorded in " & targetSheet & ".", vbInformation

' Clear form
Me.TaskDescription.Value = ""
Me.TaskType.ListIndex = -1 ' Set to no selection
Me.Collaborators.ListIndex = -1
Me.Resources.ListIndex = -1
Me.TaskPriority.ListIndex = -1
Me.CompletionDate.Value = ""
Me.StagesRequired.ListIndex = -1

End Sub

The syntax errors in the code are in the lines:

Case Is >= 4 And Is <= 9: targetSheet = "Week"
Case Is >= 10 And Is <= 20: targetSheet = "Forthnight"
Case Is >= 21 And Is <= 59: targetSheet = "Month"
Case Is >= 60 And Is <= 89: targetSheet = "QtrYear"
Case Is >= 90 And Is <= 209: targetSheet = "HalfYear"

The correct syntax should be:

Case Is >= 4 And Is <= 9
    targetSheet = "Week"
Case Is >= 10 And Is <= 20
    targetSheet = "Forthnight"
Case Is >= 21 And Is <= 59
    targetSheet = "Month"
Case Is >= 60 And Is <= 89
    targetSheet = "QtrYear"
Case Is >= 90 And Is <= 209
    targetSheet = "HalfYear"

Apologies for the confusion. The correct syntax should be:

Case Is >= 4 And Is <= 9
    targetSheet = "Week"
Case Is >= 10 And Is <= 20
    targetSheet = "Forthnight"
Case Is >= 21 And Is <= 59
    targetSheet = "Month"
Case Is >= 60 And Is <= 89
    targetSheet = "QtrYear"
Case Is >= 90 And Is <= 209
    targetSheet = "HalfYear"

Please note that in VBA, the "Select Case" statement does not support combining the comparison operators in the same line. Each line should contain only one comparison operator.
when I enter the lines one at a time I get an error Expected: expression on the 'Is' just after the And

Apologies once again for the confusion. The correct syntax should be:

Check if the value is greater than or equal to 4 and less than or equal to 9:

Case Is >= 4 And Is <= 9
    targetSheet = "Week"

Check if the value is greater than or equal to 10 and less than or equal to 20:

Case Is >= 10 And Is <= 20
    targetSheet = "Forthnight"

Check if the value is greater than or equal to 21 and less than or equal to 59:

Case Is >= 21 And Is <= 59
    targetSheet = "Month"

Check if the value is greater than or equal to 60 and less than or equal to 89:

Case Is >= 60 And Is <= 89
    targetSheet = "QtrYear"

Check if the value is greater than or equal to 90 and less than or equal to 209:

Case Is >= 90 And Is <= 209
    targetSheet = "HalfYear"

Note that each case statement should be on a separate line. I apologize for the confusion again.

' Get data from form
Dim desc As String: desc = Me.TaskDescription.Value
Dim TaskType As String: TaskType = Me.TaskType.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
Dim Collaborators As String: Collaborators = Me.Collaborators.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing

' Determine target sheet based on difference in days
Dim targetSheet As String
Select Case diffDays
    Case Is <= 3: targetSheet = "Day"
    Case Is >= 4 And Is <= 9
targetSheet = "Week"
Case Is >= 10 And Is <= 20
targetSheet = "Forthnight"

' Find next available row

' Write data to sheet
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 1).Value = desc
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 2).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(TaskType, 1).Value
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 3).Value = Me.TaskDescription
Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 4).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(Collaborators, 1).Value

Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(lastRow, 8).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(stages, 1).Value

' Display confirmation message

' Clear form

Me.Resources.ListIndex = -1

Certainly! Here's the corrected and more efficient version of your code:

Private Sub CommandButton1_Click()
    ' Get data from form
    Dim desc As String: desc = Me.TaskDescription.Value
    Dim TaskType As Long: TaskType = Me.TaskType.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
    Dim Collaborators As Long: Collaborators = Me.Collaborators.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
    Dim Resources As Long: Resources = Me.Resources.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
    Dim priority As Long: priority = Me.TaskPriority.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
    Dim compDate As Date: compDate = Me.CompletionDate.Value
    Dim stages As Long: stages = Me.StagesRequired.ListIndex + 1 ' Adjust for 0-based indexing
    
    ' Calculate difference in days
    Dim diffDays As Long: diffDays = DateDiff("d", Date, compDate)
    
    ' Determine target sheet based on difference in days
    Dim targetSheet As String
    Select Case diffDays
        Case Is <= 3: targetSheet = "Day"
        Case 4 To 9: targetSheet = "Week"
        Case 10 To 20: targetSheet = "Forthnight"
        Case 21 To 59: targetSheet = "Month"
        Case 60 To 89: targetSheet = "QtrYear"
        Case 90 To 209: targetSheet = "HalfYear"
        Case Else: targetSheet = "Year"
    End Select
    
    ' Find next available row
    Dim lastRow As Long: lastRow = Sheets(targetSheet).Cells(Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row + 1
    
    ' Write data to sheet
    With Sheets(targetSheet)
        .Cells(lastRow, 1).Value = desc
        .Cells(lastRow, 2).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(TaskType, 1).Value
        .Cells(lastRow, 3).Value = desc
        .Cells(lastRow, 4).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(Collaborators, 1).Value
        .Cells(lastRow, 5).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(Resources, 1).Value
        .Cells(lastRow, 6).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(priority, 1).Value
        .Cells(lastRow, 7).Value = compDate
        .Cells(lastRow, 8).Value = Sheets("List").Cells(stages, 1).Value
    End With
    
    ' Display confirmation message
    MsgBox "Your new task has been recorded in " & targetSheet & ".", vbInformation
    
    ' Clear form
    Me.TaskDescription.Value = ""
    Me.TaskType.ListIndex = -1 ' Set to no selection
    Me.Collaborators.ListIndex = -1
    Me.Resources.ListIndex = -1
    Me.TaskPriority.ListIndex = -1
    Me.CompletionDate.Value = ""
    Me.StagesRequired.ListIndex = -1
End Sub

These updates should resolve the syntax errors and also streamline the code for better efficiency.

In my form, the date value shown is represented as m/dd/yyyy.
How can I get the representation to show as dd/mm/yy.
I am using the VBA code below to insert the date into the form:
Private Sub UserForm_Initialize()
' Run when the form opens
Me.DateCreated.Value = Date ' Set to current date directly
End Sub

To change the date format in your UserForm, you can use the Format function in VBA. Here's how you can modify your code to display the date in the dd/mm/yy format:

Private Sub UserForm_Initialize()
    ' Run when the form opens
    Me.DateCreated.Value = Format(Date, "dd/mm/yy")
End Sub

By using the Format function with the "dd/mm/yy" format string, the Date value will be converted to the desired format before being displayed in the DateCreated field on your UserForm.

In my sheet, I enter data into rows from row 2 downwards.
I want a vba code that will do the following.
When I enter the value 'completed' into column J,
then the entire row from column A to K where I entered completed
is copied to the next available row in sheet Archive
and then the row is deleted.

Certainly! You can use the following VBA code to achieve your requirement:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Not Intersect(Target, Range("J:J")) Is Nothing Then ' Check if change occurred in column J
        Dim cell As Range
        For Each cell In Intersect(Target, Range("J:J")) ' Loop through changed cells in column J
            If UCase(cell.Value) = "COMPLETED" Then ' Check if value is "completed" (case-insensitive)
                Dim lastRow As Long
                lastRow = Sheets("Archive").Cells(Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row + 1 ' Find next available row in "Archive" sheet
                Me.Range("A" & cell.Row & ":K" & cell.Row).Copy Destination:=Sheets("Archive").Range("A" & lastRow) ' Copy row to "Archive" sheet
                Me.Rows(cell.Row).Delete ' Delete the row in current sheet
                Exit For ' Exit loop after processing one "completed" entry
            End If
        Next cell
    End If
End Sub
```

To use this code, follow these steps:
1. Right-click on the sheet name where you want this functionality and select "View Code."
2. Paste the above code into the code window that appears.
3. Save the workbook as a macro-enabled workbook (.xlsm) if it's not already saved that way.

From now on, whenever you enter the value "completed" in column J of that sheet, the entire row from column A to K where you entered "completed" will be copied to the next available row in the "Archive" sheet, and the row will be deleted from the current sheet.
is it possible to copy the values only without any formatting

Yes, it is possible to copy only the values without any formatting. You can modify the code as shown below:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Not Intersect(Target, Range("J:J")) Is Nothing Then ' Check if change occurred in column J
        Dim cell As Range
        For Each cell In Intersect(Target, Range("J:J")) ' Loop through changed cells in column J
            If UCase(cell.Value) = "COMPLETED" Then ' Check if value is "completed" (case-insensitive)
                Dim lastRow As Long
                lastRow = Sheets("Archive").Cells(Rows.Count, 1).End(xlUp).Row + 1 ' Find next available row in "Archive" sheet
                Me.Range("A" & cell.Row & ":K" & cell.Row).Copy
                Sheets("Archive").Range("A" & lastRow).PasteSpecial xlPasteValues ' Paste values only in "Archive" sheet
                Application.CutCopyMode = False ' Clear clipboard
                Me.Rows(cell.Row).Delete ' Delete the row in current sheet
                Exit For ' Exit loop after processing one "completed" entry
            End If
        Next cell
    End If
End Sub
```

This modified code uses the `PasteSpecial xlPasteValues` method to paste only the values without any formatting. It clears the clipboard after pasting and before deleting the row in the current sheet.

Note: Ensure that the "Archive" sheet exists in your workbook with the desired range to receive the copied values.
I have a sheet named planner that has links to all my other sheet in the workbook. If I delete a row in a sheet the cell address in sheet Planner loses the cell address. how can I prevent this

To prevent the cell references in the "Planner" sheet from losing their connection when a row is deleted in another sheet, you can use the `HYPERLINK` function along with the `INDIRECT` function in the "Planner" sheet.

Assuming that the cell references in the "Planner" sheet are stored in column A, you can modify the formulas in that column to use the `HYPERLINK` function. Here's an example:

```vba
=HYPERLINK("#'" & INDIRECT("SheetName") & "'!A1", "Link Text")
```

Replace "SheetName" with the actual sheet name from which you want to create the link. The `INDIRECT` function retrieves the value in the cell which contains the sheet name. The `HYPERLINK` function then creates a clickable link with the specified link text.

By using this approach, even if a row is deleted in the referenced sheet, the cell references in the "Planner" sheet will still maintain their connection.

Make sure to update the formulas accordingly for each cell reference in column A of the "Planner" sheet.

Note: The above formula assumes that all the referenced sheets have data starting from cell A1. Modify the formula accordingly if the referenced data starts from a different cell address.
The links in my sheet Planner are just simple links like this '=Day!$A2'. I want to maintain this link even if the row in sheet Day is deleted
